title: Change memory: master
summary: Public repo hygiene cleanup: removed generated launch/demo artifacts and checked-in viewer output.
body:
Repo-local change memory generated from the current cleanup diff.

What changed:
- Removed one-off marketing/demo render scripts, raw terminal recordings, generated videos, posters, and internal launch-readiness docs from the public repository.
- Removed checked-in docs/viewer output; the Pages workflow builds the viewer from mcp/viewer and current Kage artifacts during deploy.
- Removed stale memory packets that only documented deleted demo/launch artifacts.
- Kept docs/assets limited to lightweight public assets that are intentionally referenced.
- Added ignore rules for generated docs/viewer output, generated docs/assets media, and terminal transcripts.
- Updated the Pages workflow so viewer inbox data is regenerated and copied with the other Kage data files.

Why:
A public OSS repo should not carry private launch scratchwork, raw recordings, bulky generated media, committed static-site build output, or memory packets that point at removed files. Future agents should keep generated campaign assets and viewer build artifacts out of git unless they are final, lightweight, and referenced by public docs.

Verification:
- kage refresh --project . --full --json
- kage validate --project .
- npm run build --prefix mcp
- npm test --prefix mcp
- npm pack --dry-run --json from mcp/
- rg stale demo/media references returned no matches
- tracked large-file audit returned no committed source/media blobs over 1 MB

context.fact: Current branch removes public-repo garbage and generated viewer output.
context.why: Future agents should understand that generated launch/demo scratch assets and docs/viewer build output do not belong in the public OSS tree.
context.trigger: Recall when asking what changed on this branch, preparing a PR review, or resuming this work.
context.action: Keep only intentionally referenced lightweight public assets; ignore generated media/transcripts and docs/viewer output; let the Pages workflow build the viewer during deploy.
context.verification: Validated with kage refresh, kage validate, npm build/test, npm pack dry-run, and stale-reference scans.
context.risk_if_forgotten: Future agents may repeat orientation work, miss branch-specific assumptions, or ignore files touched by this change.
context.stale_when: The branch diff changes substantially, the branch is merged, or a newer change-memory packet supersedes it.

reasons: has durable trigger, rationale, issue context, or explanation
